content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://www.aau.edu/research-scholarship/featured-research-topics/even-small-businesses-minimum-wage-hikes-dont-cause", "title": "Even in Small Businesses, Minimum Wage Hikes Don't Cause Job ...", "summary": "Restaurants, retail stores and other small businesses, long thought to be vulnerable to increases in the minimum wage, generally do not cut jobs and may actually benefit when governments raise minimum pay, according to a new study co-authored at UC Berkeley. The prevailing wisdom among many business owners and policymakers is that when the minimum wage rises, smaller low-wage employers suffer more from higher labor costs and are more likely to cut jobs. But the groundbreaking new study, co-authored by Berkeley economist Michael Reich, found that small businesses can pass the costs on to consumers with little negative impact. \u201cA minimum wage increase doesn\u2019t kill jobs,\u201d said Reich, chair of UC Berkeley\u2019s Center on Wage and Employment Dynamics (CWED). \u201cIt kills job vacancies, not jobs.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Other research shows that those workers are likely to be a little more productive, as well.\u201d The federal minimum wage has been $7.25 an hour since 2009, but California and dozens of state and local governments in recent years have raised their minimu", "House of Representatives when I testified at a hearing in 2019.", "Restaurants, grocery stores and general merchandise stores account for 36% of all minimum-wage employment.", "The researchers used state-of-the art statistical methods and 30 years of employer-provided data from the U.S.", "Census to understand how some 550 changes in state and federal minimum wages between 1990 and 2019 played out in the labor market."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Other research shows that those workers are likely to be a little more productive, as well.\u201d The federal minimum wage ha"}, {"url": "https://www.nfib.com/news/analysis/minimum-wage-hikes-impact-small-business-most/", "title": "Opinion Piece Explores Impact of Minimum Wage Increase - NFIB", "summary": "And opinion article explores the unintended consequences raising the minimum wage carries, its impact on small businesses, and the greater economy. **Our take:**Data shows that minimum wage increases pose a serious barrier to entry for new businesses and entrepreneurs, leading to less dynamic and less innovative businesses. This not only hinders economic growth and the development of small businesses, but it also benefits big corporations who gain even more size and market share. **Take action:** Explain why Congress needs to oppose a $15 federal minimum wage increase. [TAKE ACTION](https://quorum.nfib.com/campaign/131656/) Minimum wage increases have taken effect in 19 states and many more cities across the country. These sweeping increases, while appearing beneficial on the surface, carry unintended consequences, and the data is clear that small businesses suffer. \u201cIt turns out that after a minimum wage increase, the number of businesses in an affected geography immediately starts to drop.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Submit ### Related Articles ![Image 27](https://www.nfib.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/March-2026-SBET-enews-320x190.png) April 15, 2026 Small Business Optimism Falls Below Average in March The March Small Business Economic Trends report sees a thre"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Submit ### Related Articles ![Image 27](https://www.nfib.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/March-2026-SBET-enews-320x190.pn"}, {"url": "https://record.umich.edu/articles/study-examines-pain-vs-gain-of-raising-minimum-wage/", "title": "Study examines pain vs. gain of raising minimum wage", "summary": "Most independent businesses can shoulder the added costs of higher minimum wages with new revenues, making only minor employment adjustments, according to new research from the University of Michigan and Carnegie Mellon University. The study, which draws on the universe of U.S. tax returns to examine the impacts of minimum wages, debunks concerns that small businesses are hit hard by minimum wage increases. Ross School of Business, who co-authored the study with Max Risch from Carnegie Mellon. \u201cOf course, these gains to workers and owners are financed by consumers, who appear fairly inelastic in their overall demand for the goods and services furnished by independent businesses affected by minimum wage policies,\u201d Rao said.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The data compare firms and workers in the states that raised their minimum wages in 2014 to those in states that didn\u2019t.", "Among restaurants, where low-earning workers make up about 16% of variable costs, as opposed to just 6% of production costs among retailers, wage hikes cause some less productive small restaurants to close.", "But they add that, at least when it comes to minimum-wage increases of the size states adopted in 2014, policymakers \u201cneed not worry about a tradeoff between redistribution and job losses or swaths of distressed independent businesses.\u201d"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: The data compare firms and workers in the states that raised their minimum wages in 2014 to those in states that didn\u2019t."}, {"url": "https://www.nelp.org/insights-research/minimum-wage-basics-employment-and-business-effects-of-minimum-wage-increases/", "title": "Employment and Business Effects of Minimum Wage Increases", "summary": "Introduction While the U.S. economy continues to see steady growth, wages have been flat or falling for much of the labor force. This dynamic has spurred the most significant wave of action to raise the minimum wage in fifty years, with momentum for significant increases at the federal, state and local levels. The growing momentum for raising the minimum wage has focused attention on the impact of higher minimum wages on employment levels.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Introduction While the U.S. economy continues to see steady growth, wages have been flat or falling for much of the labor force.", "Opponents, by contrast, generally contend that higher wages will reduce employment or slow job growth."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Introduction While the U.S. economy continues to see steady growth, wages have been flat or falling for much of the labo"}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2211335526000306", "title": "Effects of non-minimum wages on health: A narrative literature review of short-and long-run studies using causal inference or longitudinal data in high-income\u00a0\u2026", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0277953624010803", "title": "The unintended effects of a large minimum wage increase on health: Evidence from South Korea", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}]}
